issues.body: The script currently extracts 5 GUI aliases and 4 CLI aliases, but only rejects counts below 2 for each class. If formatting or structure changes make the regex miss up to three GUI entries or two CLI entries, the check still succeeds and those site-referenced assets are never checked against the release list. That directly violates the stated fail-closed invariant and can publish a release with silent-404 buttons. The diff also adds no regression test demonstrating that partial extraction fails.
issues.fix_hint: Make extraction structurally robust or validate an independently derived complete expected set rather than weak count floors. At minimum, add a test fixture covering every current GUI/CLI reference and mutations where one or more references cease matching; each partial-extraction mutation must fail rather than approve a reduced contract. Avoid merely hard-coding today's counts without a mechanism that remains synchronized with intentional site changes.
issues.title: Extraction can silently lose aliases while the fail-closed guard still passes

summary: The alias staging itself matches the current download component, but the purported fail-closed contract is not actually fail-closed. Its non-vacuity thresholds permit the extractor to lose multiple currently required aliases and still pass, so a source refactor can reintroduce silent 404 download buttons. No test is added to exercise this load-bearing failure mode.
